commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer override before normal merge review.; Cleared: The diff only adds a small TypeScript helper and regression test; it does not touch CI, dependencies, secrets, package resolution, or code download/execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The branch extracts embedded attempt trajectory flush cleanup into a small helper and adds a focused fake-timer regression test for nonfatal flush timeouts.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +47. Total +73 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence current-main failure remains: source inspection shows the cleanup helper resolves after timeout and the latest release docs say the flush timeout logs and continues. The PR provides deterministic fake-timer coverage for the reported failure mode, but I did not run a real I/O-pressure reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer override before normal merge review.

target.title: test(agents): cover nonfatal trajectory flush timeout
target.body:
Fixes #88520.

## Summary

- extract the embedded-attempt trajectory flush cleanup call into a small helper
- add a regression test proving a stalled trajectory flush times out without rejecting attempt cleanup
- keep the existing timeout logging and flush-state details intact

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: The issue reported that a `pi-trajectory-flush` timeout could abort the agent run. Current `upstream/main` already uses the nonfatal cleanup timeout helper for `openclaw-trajectory-flush`; this patch locks that behavior at the embedded-attempt call boundary.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw-88520`, branch `fix/trajectory-flush-nonfatal-88520`, commit `6c80fa8f95`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-trajectory-flush-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/run-cleanup-timeout.test.ts
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: The cleanup helper and attempt-boundary regression tests passed, and the outgoing commit author was verified:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  12 passed (12)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.06s
6c80fa8f95 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> test: cover nonfatal trajectory flush timeout
```

Observed result after fix: A stalled trajectory recorder `flush()` resolves from the cleanup wrapper after the timeout, logs `agent cleanup timed out` with pending write details, and does not reject the attempt cleanup path.

What was not tested: I did not reproduce the reporter's macOS I/O pressure event; the regression uses fake timers and a stalled flush promise to cover the reported failure mode deterministically.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
